description: Unstuck the hold window by executing a concrete conversion-surface repair: committed and deployed Codeberg-primary CTA ordering fix that was sitting in git stash across two docs pages.

summary.change: Codeberg-primary link ordering — Codeberg CTA appears before GitHub in link lists, navbar pills, and meta descriptions on both pages
summary.prior_state: git stash (uncommitted fix from prior blog deploy run), .ruby-version downgrade discarded
repairs_applied_in_run: Committed only the two docs CTA fixes
Verified CTA live on both pages

hold_window_rationale: Both an active_loop_prompt_repair and post_hold_reentry_contract_repair already exist. Rule forbids spending another slot on rerun/prompt tweaks. Chose a concrete runtime/process repair with code deploy instead.
highest_leverage_rationale: ralphworkflow.com docs pages are ranked, indexed, and receiving organic traffic. Fixing Codeberg-primary link ordering on these pages is a direct conversion optimization — it points evaluators arriving from search to the primary repo instead of the mirror. This is a concrete, verifiable improvement with zero human gate.
pypi_note: PyPI README CTAs remain stale (0.8.7, no Codeberg CTA, 1,498 downloads/month). Blocked by TWINE_PASSWORD unset — requires human action or credential setup. Not executable from this environment.
